mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with p > 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal spending and stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by > 1%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates with negative 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, prompting equity investors to downgrade near-term earnings forecasts and driving negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to precede negative SPX returns, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge with Rank IC < 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth potential, supporting higher corporate profit margins and positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods with p > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% alongside rising inflation, monetary policy tightens aggressively to combat price pressures, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL rises, or the threshold condition fails to separate winning and losing periods with t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue fundamentals, creating immediate positive sentiment and upward price pressure on the S&P 500 over a 5-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs in a live backtest.
